reasoning:
The monthly CPI year-over-year figures for 2025 so far are:
January: 3.0%
February: 2.8%
March: 2.4%
April: 2.3%
May: 2.4%
June: 2.7%
July: 2.7%
August: 2.9%
September: 3.0%

For this market to resolve YES, inflation would have to hit at least 4.1% in the 12-month period ending in any month of 2025. It has not hit the required 4.1% threshold in any month through September. At the latest available mark (September), inflation was 3.0%.

This means that for the outcome to be YES, 12-month inflation would need to jump from 3.0% in September to 4.1% in October, November, or December 2025. To achieve a 1.1 percentage point increase in year-over-year inflation in just 3 months would require astronomical month-over-month inflation (especially since Q4 2024 saw MoM increases of around 0.3-0.4%, providing a fairly normal base), which is essentially unprecedented without exceptional economic shocks like massive oil spikes. While inflation has reaccelerated slightly due to tariffs and drought, professional economic forecasts do not anticipate a >4% figure.

Because it would require an extreme and wildly unexpected spike, the true probability is likely very low. I am assigning the lowest boundary available (2%).
